{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = {{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]][[Category:qq]]

{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = {{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = "When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 {{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= {{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:11 6 4]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]]] The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} ]] ]] ]] ]] ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6 4]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]

{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} ]] ]] ]] ]] ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6 4]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]

The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}}{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]

{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]

However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]

{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Tenson kōrin|Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]]

The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Tenson kōrin|Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} [[cs:Ugajafukiaezu]] [[es:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[fa:اوگایافوکی‌آئه‌زو]] [[fr:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ja:ウガヤフキアエズ]] [[ko:우가야후키아에즈]] [[mni:ꯎꯒꯌꯐꯨꯀꯤꯑꯏꯓꯨ]] [[pl:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ru:Угаяфукиаэдзу но Микото]] [[simple:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[th:อูงายาฟูกิอาเอซุ]] [[uk:Уґаяфукі-аедзу но мікото]] [[vi:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[zh:鸕鷀草葺不合尊]] [[zh_yue:鸕鶿草葺不合尊]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Tenson kōrin|Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:11 6 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} [[cs:Ugajafukiaezu]] [[es:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[fa:اوگایافوکی‌آئه‌زو]] [[fr:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ja:ウガヤフキアエズ]] [[ko:우가야후키아에즈]] [[mni:ꯎꯒꯌꯐꯨꯀꯤꯑꯏꯓꯨ]] [[pl:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ru:Угаяфукиаэдзу но Микото]] [[simple:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[th:อูงายาฟูกิอาเอซุ]] [[uk:Уґаяфукі-аедзу но мікото]] [[vi:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[zh:鸕鷀草葺不合尊]] [[zh-yue:鸕鶿草葺不合尊]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Tenson kōrin|Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:11 6 3]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} [[cs:Ugajafukiaezu]] [[es:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[fa:اوگایافوکی‌آئه‌زو]] [[fr:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ja:ウガヤフキアエズ]] [[ko:우가야후키아에즈]] [[mni:ꯎꯒꯌꯐꯨꯀꯤꯑꯏꯓꯨ]] [[pl:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ru:Угаяфукиаэдзу но Микото]] [[simple:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[th:อูงายาฟูกิอาเอซุ]] [[uk:Уґаяфукі-аедзу но мікото]] [[vi:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[zh:鸕鷀草葺不合尊]] [[zh-yue:鸕鶿草葺不合尊]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] to [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology and Shinto}} {{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | image = File:Ugaya otokowa.png | birth_place = [[Japan]] | birth_date = 735 BC | death_date = 640 BC (aged 95) | death_place = [[Japan]] | consort = [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]] | parents = [[Hoori]] (father)
[[Toyotama-hime]] (mother) | children = {{Plainlist| *[[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] * [[Itsuse no Mikoto]] *[[Inahi no Mikoto]] *[[Mikeiri no Mikoto]] }} }} {{Nihongo|'''Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto'''|鵜葺草葺不合命}}{{cite book|last2=Heldt|first2=Gustav|author1=Ō no Yasumaro|author-link1=Ō no Yasumaro|title=The Kojiki : an account of ancient matters|date=2014|isbn=9780231163897}} is a [[Shinto]] ''[[kami]]'', and is in [[Japanese mythology]], the father of Japan's first Emperor, [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. ==Nomenclature and story== [[File:Chikanobu-Toyotama-hime.jpg|thumb|right|Toyotama-hime giving birth to Ugayafukiaezu by turning herself into a [[Wani (dragon)|''wani'']] in an 1886 illustration]] In the ''[[Kojiki]]'', his name appears as {{Nihongo||天津日高日子波限建鵜葺草葺不合命|Amatsuhiko Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}, and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo||彦波瀲武鸕鶿草葺不合尊|Hiko Nagisatake Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto}}. [[:en:Basil Hall Chamberlain|Basil Hall Chamberlain]] glossed the ''Kojiki'' name as "His Augustness Heaven's-Sun-Height-Prince-Wave-limit-Brave-Cormorant-Thatch-Meeting-Incompletely". 'no Mikoto' here is an honorific, denoting divinity or royalty. Ugayafukiaezu was a child of [[Hoori]], the son of [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], who was sent down by [[Amaterasu]] to govern the earth ([[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni]]) (believed to be equivalent to Japan), and of [[Toyotama-hime]], a daughter of [[Ryūjin]], the dragon ''[[kami]]'' of the sea. Although Toyotama-hime became pregnant at the undersea palace of [[Ryūgū-jō]], she opted not to bear the child in the ocean and decided to head to shore. On the shore, her parents attempted to build a house in which she could give birth, and attempted to construct the roof with feathers of the [[Great cormorant|cormorant]] instead of [[Cladium|saw grass]]. However, while they were finishing the roof, she went into labor. {{blockquote|And so just as she was about to give birth, she spoke to her husband, saying:

"When their time draws near, people of other lands all give birth in the form of their homeland. So I will now give birth in my original form. Please, I beg you, do not look at me!"

Now, thinking these words strange, he sneaked up and peered in at her just as she was about to give birth.

She had become an enormous sea beast many arm spans in length that was twisting and slithering around on its stomach.

In shock and fright at the sight of her, he immediately fled far away.}} In shame, Toyotama-hime fled, leaving behind her newborn, whom she called Ugayafukiaezu. The roof of the birthing hut had not been completely thatched (''fukiaezu'') with cormorant feathers (''ugaya'') when his mother gave birth to him, which explains his name. Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Tenson kōrin|Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:11 6 3]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} [[cs:Ugajafukiaezu]] [[es:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[fa:اوگایافوکی‌آئه‌زو]] [[fr:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ja:ウガヤフキアエズ]] [[ko:우가야후키아에즈]] [[mni:ꯎꯒꯌꯐꯨꯀꯤꯑꯏꯓꯨ]] [[pl:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ru:Угаяфукиаэдзу но Микото]] [[simple:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[th:อูงายาฟูกิอาเอซุ]] [[uk:Уґаяфукі-аедзу но мікото]] [[vi:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[zh:鸕鷀草葺不合尊]] [[zh-yue:鸕鶿草葺不合尊]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

Later, when Ugayafukiaezu reached adulthood, he married his aunt, [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-hime]], and they had four children: [[Itsuse no Mikoto|Hikoitsuse]], [[Inahi no Mikoto|Inai]], [[Mikeiri no Mikoto|Mikeirinu]], and Hikohohodemi (later [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]). Mikeirinu traveled to [[Tokoyo no kuni]], the "Everworld", and Inai went into the ocean to be with his mother. The eldest and youngest set forth to rule the land and while they did so together for a time, after Hikoitsuse died, their youngest became the first ruler. ==Genealogy== Ugayafukiaezu is in the [[Tenson kōrin|Three generations of Hyuga]], a time period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}} {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}} ==References== {{reflist|2|refs={{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Kokushi Daijiten | title = 鵜葺草葺不合命 | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = ja | trans-title = Ugayafukiaezu | oclc = 683276033 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}}} ==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=161 Encyclopedia of Shinto] {{jmyth navbox long}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:11 6 3]] }} [[sn:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[en:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto}} [[cs:Ugajafukiaezu]] [[es:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[fa:اوگایافوکی‌آئه‌زو]] [[fr:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ja:ウガヤフキアエズ]] [[ko:우가야후키아에즈]] [[mni:ꯎꯒꯌꯐꯨꯀꯤꯑꯏꯓꯨ]] [[pl:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[ru:Угаяфукиаэдзу но Микото]] [[simple:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[th:อูงายาฟูกิอาเอซุ]] [[uk:Уґаяфукі-аедзу но мікото]] [[vi:Ugayafukiaezu]] [[zh:鸕鷀草葺不合尊]] [[zh-yue:鸕鶿草葺不合尊]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]]
